2606.27302.title: AI Healthcare Chatbots as Information Infrastructure: A Large-Scale Study of User-Reported Breakdowns
2606.27302.paper_summary: AI healthcare chatbots are increasingly used to support health information seeking and self-management, yet their performance and impact on users remains to be studied. This study examines over 15,000 user reviews from 59 AI healthcare chatbot apps to explore how these systems function in everyday informational and emotional contexts. Topic modeling and interpretive analysis identify three recurring breakdowns: access barriers and service unreliability, user experience and interaction quality, and billing and customer support issues. Privacy and security concerns are associated with the most negative experiences. By framing AI healthcare chatbots as information infrastructures, our findings highlight how failures in access, usability, and trust affect users, offering actionable insights for designers, policymakers, and information professionals aiming to improve digital health systems.
2606.27302.paper_summary_zh: AI 醫療聊天機器人越來越多地被用來支持健康信息的尋求和自我管理，但它們的性能和對用戶的影響仍需進一步研究。這項研究分析了來自 59 款 AI 醫療聊天機器人應用的超過 15,000 條用戶評論，以探索這些系統在日常信息和情感背景下的運作方式。主題建模和詮釋性分析識別出三個反覆出現的問題：訪問障礙和服務不可靠性、用戶體驗和互動質量，以及計費和客戶支持問題。隱私和安全問題與最負面的體驗相關聯。通過將 AI 醫療聊天機器人框架視為信息基礎設施，我們的研究結果突顯了訪問性、可用性和信任的失敗如何影響用戶，為設計師、政策制定者和信息專業人士提供了可行的見解，以改善數字健康系統。
2606.27302.author: Muhammad Hassan et.al.
2606.27302.authors: Muhammad Hassan, Ramazan Yener, Ece Gumusel, Masooda Bashir
